spark-comet:position-deletes:v2.notes: Comet accelerates Parquet scans only; all writes and DML fall back to vanilla Spark, which provides the underlying feature support.
caveats: Writes/DML are executed by vanilla Spark, not the native engine

spark-comet:position-deletes:v3.notes: Comet accelerates Parquet scans only; all writes and DML fall back to vanilla Spark, which provides the underlying feature support.
caveats: Writes/DML are executed by vanilla Spark, not the native engine
Deletion vectors (V3) require Iceberg 1.6+ and Spark 3.5+ or 4.0+

spark-comet:equality-deletes:v2.notes: Comet accelerates Parquet scans only; all writes and DML fall back to vanilla Spark, which provides the underlying feature support.
caveats: Writes/DML are executed by vanilla Spark, not the native engine

spark-comet:copy-on-write:v3.notes: Comet accelerates Parquet scans only; all writes and DML fall back to vanilla Spark, which provides the underlying feature support.
caveats: Writes/DML are executed by vanilla Spark, not the native engine

spark-comet:schema-evolution:v2.notes: Read path is natively accelerated by Apache DataFusion Comet (native_iceberg_compat Parquet scan, including complex types on Spark 4.0). ORC/Avro and unsupported cases fall back to vanilla Spark. Iceberg feature support is inherited from Spark.
caveats: Only Parquet scans are accelerated natively; ORC/Avro fall back to vanilla Spark

spark-comet:schema-evolution:v3.notes: Read path is natively accelerated by Apache DataFusion Comet (native_iceberg_compat Parquet scan, including complex types on Spark 4.0). ORC/Avro and unsupported cases fall back to vanilla Spark. Iceberg feature support is inherited from Spark.
caveats: Only Parquet scans are accelerated natively; ORC/Avro fall back to vanilla Spark

spark-comet:column-default-values:v2.notes: Column default values (initial-default, write-default) are a V3 feature

spark-comet:column-default-values:v3.notes: Comet native Parquet scan support for this V3 type is limited; reads fall back to vanilla Spark, which provides the underlying support.
caveats: Reads of this type fall back to vanilla Spark (limited native Comet acceleration)
No Spark SQL DDL syntax for setting Iceberg column defaults
Requires Iceberg 1.6+ with format-version 3

spark-comet:nanosecond-timestamps:v2.notes: Nanosecond timestamps are a V3 feature

spark-comet:nanosecond-timestamps:v3.notes: Comet native Parquet scan support for this V3 type is limited; reads fall back to vanilla Spark, which provides the underlying support.
caveats: Reads of this type fall back to vanilla Spark (limited native Comet acceleration)
Microsecond TIMESTAMP remains the default; only switch when nanosecond precision is required
Requires format-version 3

spark-comet:hidden-partitioning:v2.notes: Read path is natively accelerated by Apache DataFusion Comet (native_iceberg_compat Parquet scan, including complex types on Spark 4.0). ORC/Avro and unsupported cases fall back to vanilla Spark. Iceberg feature support is inherited from Spark.
caveats: Only Parquet scans are accelerated natively; ORC/Avro fall back to vanilla Spark

caveats: Reads of this type fall back to vanilla Spark (limited native Comet acceleration)
Multi-source-column transforms are a new V3 capability with evolving Spark support
Requires Spark 4.0+ for full V3 transform support

spark-comet:statistics:v2.notes: Read path is natively accelerated by Apache DataFusion Comet (native_iceberg_compat Parquet scan, including complex types on Spark 4.0). ORC/Avro and unsupported cases fall back to vanilla Spark. Iceberg feature support is inherited from Spark.
caveats: Only Parquet scans are accelerated natively; ORC/Avro fall back to vanilla Spark

caveats: Only Parquet scans are accelerated natively; ORC/Avro fall back to vanilla Spark
Bloom filters must be explicitly enabled per column via table properties
Not enabled by default
